segments.text:
Open up
Open up
Heavy
Heavy mirrors
Glass that never shows the marrow
I walked three miles down a road too narrow
Wasted
Wasted days
Wasted nights
Tearing through the fabric of the frame
Forgetting that I carried His holy name
Cold water
Cold spirit
Cold breath
Wash the ink away
Wash the mask away
Wash the doubt away
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
To the garden where you started
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
Back home
Back home
Open up
Open up
Pure light
Pure light
Dust
Settling down
Finding peace within the quiet grain
Giving up the power and the constant pain
Surrender
Everything
Everything
Let the clay go soft inside His hands
Walking out of these shifting sands
New heart
New skin
New life
Come as you were
Come as you were
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
To the garden where you started
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
Back home
Back home
No more running
No more hiding
No more fighting the design
He is the root
He is the truth
He is the vine
I see the sunrise
I see the sunrise
I see the sunrise
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
To the garden where you started
He is calling you back home
Calling you back home
Back home
Back home
Open up
Open up
Pure light
Pure light
Back home
Back home
Back home
